frameworks.name: mouse-tracking
frameworks.one_line: hand movement trajectories as continuous index of multiple response activations

frameworks.name: reaction time
frameworks.one_line: traditional discrete temporal measure benchmarked against continuous mouse-tracking

frameworks.name: brain imaging
frameworks.one_line: future mapping of mouse-tracking signals to neural representations

core_question: How can mouse-tracking expose the micro-structure and millisecond time-course of real-time decisions across psychological science?
key_assumptions: hand movements reflect multiple simultaneous response activations
continuous motor output is informative about latent decision dynamics
millisecond-resolution trajectories index component processes of choice
key_findings: mouse-tracking has become widely adopted across psychological science in the past decade
recent work shows the method is broadly applicable beyond its early debate-specific use
the method exposes component processes and millisecond time-course of real-time decisions
interpretations: mouse-tracking is a general-purpose, theory-informing measure with broader value than originally appreciated
comparisons with reaction time and other temporally sensitive methods position mouse-tracking as a complementary continuous measure
limitations_noted: theoretical understanding of mouse-tracking methodology itself still needs improvement
